meaning: vt.打...的屁股,拍击,鞭策...前进,飞跑vi.飞跑n.一巴掌,出色的人(或东西)
examples: At our center, we see many parents who spank their children to teach them not to hit their siblings.
The other two are "good fathers" in that they have steady jobs, pay maintenance, make their children laugh and do not spank them.
Overall, there was a large generational shift in which the second generation of parents was much less likely to spank than their own parents.
When I had to spank the stuffed animals for being bad, I became the mean teacher.
The ball was fed out to the left-flank and the veteran Feyenoord left-back had time to look up and use his trusty left foot to spank an unstoppable 35-yard shot into the top corner.
